Ui the character's chutzpah rll is successful, this power gives the user a +5 bonus on all subsequent chutzpah skill rolls. (except spurious logic, of course) against NPCs. The greatest risk in using this power is that victims invariably return to normal after the effect wears off. They remember thelr abnot- mal behavior and realize that they have been hosed. Therefore, if this power is used ‘epeatedly on any subject, particularly other player characters, the mutant is running a con~ Siderable risk of revealing possession of the mutation. ‘When the poner is used on player characters (either by a player character or by an NPC), the gamemaster should prepare notes for the players, explaining that they feel inexplicably friendly and cooperative, that they are eager to share their deepest secrets, and that they should roleplay this insidious attack of trust, and ood will until further notice, No hint ‘should be given about the source of the feel- ing When the effect has dissipated, they should be given notes that their feelings have retuned t0 normal, 
WH Deep Probe The character can reach deep inside the mind of another character and extract infor: mation from unwilling subjects, The mutant ‘must be in physical, lesh-tolesh contact with the victim. The experience is painful and ex hausting for both parties — shock, nausea, and severe headaches are common, In yare terms, both character and victim suffer a “wound.” They remain “wounded” until they've had several hours of rest or a nice nap. Given the commonly-rumored existence of psionic mutant powers, most subjects will realize that their minds have been probed. However, their only recollection of the ex perience will be a feeling of great pain, then ‘Unconsciousness. ‘The deeper and more obscure the informa- tion for which the user is probing, theless like- ly the success of the probe. The gamemaster makes a power roll with appropriate skill ‘modifiers. Ifthe roll is successful, the prober obtains most of the information he wants in, faicly coherent form (little extraneous informa tion, more-or-less in proper and logical se- ‘quence, without significant gaps or omissions, atc) lithe roll is unsuccessful, the prober gets in complete and garbled information, liberally sprinkled with irelevant childhood memories, stream-oF.cansciousness sense impressions in- verted word order, free association chains, vaguely related but uninteresting anecdotes, and the like, This is a great chance fo annoy Your players by salting a few useful tidbits in- to heaps of word salad. ‘A few examples to illustrate ease of power 
* probing a sentry for a password — very easy, ‘= probing a technician for an operating pro- cedure — easy * probing a Troubleshooter for memories con- cerning a region visited ten years ago — difficult 
PARANOIA 
* probing for a serial number seen on a weapon for a few moments five years in the past fa memory nat even accessible tothe con- scious mind of the subject) — real difficult 
Hl ctectroshock 
The characteris extremely resistant to elec trical shocks stungun shocks in particular) and can deliver one electric shock per round to any fone victim within afive-meter radius, The et- fect is equivalent to that of a stungun. To resist the effect of an electric shock, the character must make a successful power roll. ‘The gamemaster should modify the attribute number for particularly large (high tension wite) of small (Walkman battery) voltages. ‘To deliver a shock o a Victim, the character ‘must make a power rll successful, the vie- tim is zapped stungun-style. If unsuccessful, something goes wrong — the character stuns himself, or blows out his communications gear, ‘or makes everyone's hair stand on end, oF broadcasts in the shortwave band. 
WH Empathy ‘This character has a sort of hind-brain telepathy — he can read a victim's emotional brain activity and project his own emotions to. manipulate the victim’s emotional state Reading a victin’s emotions is particularly useful when trying to detect larimprogress; ‘successful roll_ may reveal the intent to ‘mislead. When trying to assess anothers reac- tion, a successful empathy power roll entitles the User to GM hints. When using chutzpah skills, a successful empathy roll means a. +5 modifier to the skill number. The most effective use of empathy i to pro- Ject sttong emotions on a victim in order to interfere with his ability to reason and make judgments. The empath works himself up in- to a frenzy, then projects his emotions onto his victim, The victim is overcome by a tem- porary obsessive need to indulge himself in dramatic actions which express the emotion = sobbing, screaming, cowering, cackling maniacally, smashing things — a real oppor- {unity for the hystericallysinclined roleplayer. Ie the power rol is successful, the victim is a fected; the more successful the roll, the more exaggerated and obsessive the performance. Iv the rol fails, the vietim is unaffected, and the empath himself may become obsessed, suffering temporary insanity proportional to hhow unsuccessful the roll was This kil is mast reliable when used on an individual, Atempting empathy with a crowd is less accurate and reliable, and the empath ‘s more likely to get caught up in the emotions of the crowe with an unsuccessful power roll ‘The gamemaster should take the player aside, explain the mood ofthe crowd, and encourage him in his roleplaying of a victim of mob psychology. 
I Energy Field This power produces a field that absorbs energy and radiates it uniformly from the sur face of the field as heat and light. The field follows the extemal contours of the body, 
‘extending about six inches from the skin sur- face. In game terms, this acts like "AII3” ar- ‘mor; that is, the darage number of any attack con the character is reduced by 3, regardless ‘of weapon type. The field usually lasts no more than a ‘minute, and leaves the user exhausted and in shock — the equivalent of wounded — until he has had a few hours of rest or a nice nap. “The field is also unfortunately rather showy — it sparkles and shimmers, and emits brief, blinding flashes of light and heat when struck by melee or ranged weapons or by other sources of energy (Le., a speeding autocan), or When the character runs into a wall or falls, from a great height. This field is subtle, complex, and unreliable in its workings. When the power roll is unsuc- 
cessful, lots of nasty things can happen. Sometimes it reflects all energy — light, for ex: ample, leaving the character blind. Sometimes the energy is radiated from a small fraction of the surface — from the feet, for example, burm- ing a hole in the floor — or the energy is radiated from the inner surface, parboiling the ‘mutant. Note that when the mutant fires a ranged ‘weapon from inside the field — when the bar- rel does not protrude through the field — the ‘weapon's projectile or beam may be reflected from the inner surface. Note that sometimes the field fluctuates wildly, expanding from then contracting toward the body. 
Wi Hypersenses ‘This power amplifies the five senses, The ‘eyes can see clearly for great distances, magnify greatly, and function well in near darkness or under blinding illumination. The ‘ears can hear very minute sounds, or sounds at great distances. The skin is extremely sen Sitive, alert to changes in temperature and humility ai pressure, and so on. The nose is acute, detecting the subtlest smells even among overpowering odors. The taste buds can finely distinguish between faniliar substances, even substances which have no distinctive taste to normal humans. This power also gives the mutant superior discrimination in focusing on one aspect of ‘perception and ignoring the rest — for ex- ‘ample, picking out one voice from an unruly ‘crowd and distinguishing what is being said. “This is what the power does when it works right — when the power roll is successful. ‘When the power roll is unsuccessful, things ‘are not so swell. Maybe everything seems to be turned up too loud. Maybe normal human ‘odors become appallingly overpowering. Maybe the character begins hallucinating — “My skin's on fret My tongue's exploding! My ears are melting! Aiieee!” It ain't easy being a mutant
